events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: PVC flue slope back-pitched on a condensing furnace. This is a non-compliant and installed-defective condition. Flue must slope upward toward the vent terminal (or per OEM, typically 1/4" per foot upward) to drain condensate back to the furnace. Back-pitch traps condensate, causing blockage, flue gas spillage, CO hazard. High/critical risk. Fail. Immediate controls: shut down furnace, correct slope, support/repipe.

"rationale": "A back-pitched condensing furnace PVC vent traps acidic condensate, can block the flue, and may cause combustion product spillage including CO. This violates IRC M1801 and NFPA 54 vent slope/drainage requirements and is unsafe for continued operation.",
